For if it in no way participated in unity, neither would the whole be one whole, nor would each of the many of which the manifold consists be one—for each of them would itself be a manifold, and this to infinity. And each of these infinites would itself be infinitely infinite. For a manifold that participates in unity in no way, neither as a whole nor in respect of each of its parts, would be infinite in every respect and in respect of every one of its parts. For each of the many, whatever one you take, will be either one or not-one; and if not-one, then either many or nothing. But if each is nothing, the whole composed of nothings is nothing. And if each is many, each will be composed of infinitely many infinities. But this is impossible. Therefore every manifold participates in unity.
orig: Πᾶν πλῆθος μετέχει πῃ τοῦ ἑνός.
title: Elements of Theology 1-3: Every Manifold Participates in Unity
author: Proclus

en: All that participates in unity is both one and not-one. For if it is not the One itself—since it participates in unity by having unity, not by being unity—it has acquired unity by participation and therefore has been affected in respect of its unity-having. But what has been affected in respect of its participation in unity, insofar as it has been affected, is not-one. And since it is not-one but has come to participate in unity, it is at once both one and not-one. It is not one in the same respect in which it is not-one, but is one by participation and not-one in its own nature.
orig: Πᾶν τὸ τοῦ ἑνὸς μετέχον καὶ ἕν ἐστι καὶ οὐχ ἕν.
title: Elements of Theology 1-3: Every Manifold Participates in Unity
author: Proclus

en: All that becomes one does so by participating in the One, and derives its unity from the One. For if what is not essentially one is nevertheless one, it has surely become one by having been affected in a certain way and by having acquired unity. And to acquire unity is to participate in unity. Therefore, if it is one insofar as it participates, and what participates derives what it participates in from its source, then the unity of what is unified derives from the One, which is the first principle of unity.
orig: Πᾶν τὸ γινόμενον ἓν τῇ τοῦ ἑνὸς μεθέξει ἓν γίνεται.
title: Elements of Theology 1-3: Every Manifold Participates in Unity
author: Proclus

en: For the purpose of our journey is the contemplation of that which truly exists, which is the intelligible. The soul must withdraw from all that is foreign to it, and the body and its affections are foreign to the soul. We must therefore become strangers to the body and its life, if we wish to achieve contact with the intelligible and truly real.
orig: τέλος γὰρ τῆς πορείας ἡ τῶν ὄντως Ὄντων θεωρία, τουτέστι τῶν νοητῶν
title: On Abstinence I: The Soul's Return
author: Porphyry

en: Since to each thing there is something proper and something foreign, and what is proper nourishes and preserves while what is foreign corrupts and destroys—we must distinguish what is proper to the soul and what is foreign. What is proper to the soul is the contemplation of true being and the intelligible order. What is foreign is attachment to the body, to sensation, and to the passions that arise from the body.
orig: ἐπεὶ ἑκάστῳ τὸ μὲν οἰκεῖον τὸ δὲ ἀλλότριον
title: On Abstinence I: The Soul's Return
author: Porphyry

en: The philosopher, then, must separate himself from all that connects him to the body—not by violence but by the gradual withdrawal of desire. For the passions are bonds that bind the soul to the corporeal. And each pleasure is a nail that fastens the soul to the body, making the soul believe that what the body tells it is true. The soul that has been purified of these attachments and freed from the body’s influence turns naturally toward the intelligible, as a thing returns to its proper place when the impediment is removed.
orig: δεῖ οὖν τὸν φιλόσοφον ἀφαιρεῖν ἑαυτὸν ἀπὸ πάντων τῶν συναπτόντων πρὸς τὸ σῶμα
title: On Abstinence I: The Soul's Return
author: Porphyry

en: The end is not to be without sin but to become God. It is not merely moral perfection that we seek but likeness to the divine—and not merely likeness but, insofar as is possible for human nature, union with it. The return of the soul to God is the natural completion of its journey, as the return of a stream to the sea.
orig: τέλος γὰρ οὐ τὸ ἀναμάρτητον ἀλλὰ τὸ θεὸν γενέσθαι.
title: On Abstinence I: The Soul's Return
author: Porphyry

en:
Hear me, all you hallowed beings, both high and low of Heimdall’s children. You wish, Odin, that I recount the ancient tales of beings, the earliest I remember.
        I remember giants born in ancient times, those who long ago reared me. Nine worlds I remember, nine wood-ogresses, the glorious tree of fate beneath the earth.
orig:
Hlióðs bið ek allar helgar kindir, meiri ok minni mögu Heimdallar; viltu at ek, Valföðr, vel fyr telja forn spöll fira, þau er fremst um man.
        Ek man jötna ár um borna, þá er forðum mik fœdda höfðu; níu man ek heima, níu íviðjur, mjötvið mæran fyr mold neðan.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en:
In the beginning there was nothing: neither sand nor sea, nor cold waves. No earth existed, nor heaven above. There was only Ginnungagap—the Yawning Void—and grass was nowhere.
        Before the sons of Bur raised up the lands, they who shaped the glorious Middle-Earth. The sun shone from the south on the stones of the hall; then the ground was grown with green plants.
orig:
Ár var alda þat er ekki var, var-a sandr né sær né svalar unnir; jörð fannsk æva né upphiminn, gap var ginnunga en gras hvergi.
        Áðr Burs synir bjöðum um ypðu, þair er Miðgarð mæran skópu; sól skín sunnan á salar steina, þá var grund gróin grœnum lauki.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en:
I know an ash tree that stands, called Yggdrasil, a tall tree, watered with white clay. From there come the dews that fall in the valleys. It stands ever green above the Well of Fate.
        From there come three maidens, deep in knowledge, from the lake that stands beneath the tree. One is called Urð (“Fate”), another Verðandi (“Becoming”), the third Skuld (“Obligation”). They carved on wooden slips, they laid down laws, they chose the lives of the children of mankind.
orig:
Ask veit ek standa, heitir Yggdrasill, hár baðmr, ausinn hvita auri; þaðan koma döggvar þars er í dala falla, stendr æ yfir grœnn Urðar brunni.
        Þaðan koma meyjar márg vitandi þrír ór þim sal er und þolli stendr; Urð hétu eina, aðra Verðandi, skáru á skiði, Skuld ina þriðju.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en:
Yggdrasil trembles, the ash standing, the old tree groans, and the giant breaks free. All are in fear on the roads of Hel, before the kin of Surt swallows it.
        What ails the Aesir? What ails the elves? All Jötunheim groans. The gods are in council. The dwarves moan before their doors of stone, wise ones of the cliff-walls. Do you know yet, or what?
        Hrym drives from the east, holds his shield before him. Jörmungandr writhes in giant fury. The serpent churns the waves. The eagle screams, tears corpses pale-beaked. Naglfar breaks free.
orig: Skelfr Yggdrasils askr standandi, ymr it aldna tré, en jötunn losnar... Hrymr ekr austan, hefisk lind fyrir, snýsk Jörmungandr í jötunmóði.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en:
The sun turns black. Earth sinks into the sea. The bright stars fall from the sky. Flame rages against the World Tree. The heat soars high against heaven itself.
        She sees rising a second time earth from the sea, eternally green. Waterfalls flow. The eagle flies above, hunting fish on the mountain.
        The Aesir meet on Idavoll and speak of the mighty World Serpent. They remember the great events and the ancient runes of the Mighty One.
orig:
Sól tér sortna, sígr fold í mar, hverfa af himni heiðar stjörnur; gæsir eimi ok aldrnari, leikr hár hiti við himin sjálfan.
        Sér hon upp koma öðru sinni jörð ór ægi, iðjagrœna; falla forsar, flýgr örn yfir, sá er á fjalli fiska veiðir.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en:
There the wondrous golden game-pieces shall be found in the grass, those which they possessed in the earliest days.
        Unsown shall the fields bear fruit. All ills shall be healed. Baldr shall come. Baldr and Höðr shall dwell in Hropt’s battle-hall, the shrines of the slain gods. Do you know yet, or what?
        Then Hœnir shall choose the prophetic wooden slip, and the sons of two brothers shall dwell broadly in the wide Wind-Home. Do you know yet, or what?
        She sees a hall standing, fairer than the sun, roofed with gold, at Gimlé. There shall worthy people dwell and enjoy delights for all ages.
orig: Þar munu eftir undrsamligar gullnar töflur í grasi finnask... Ser hon sal standa, sólu fegra, gulli þakðan, á Gimlé; þar skulu dyggar drottir byggja ok um aldrdaga yndi njóta.
title: Voluspa: The Seeress's Prophecy
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en: I know that I hung on the wind-swept tree, nine full nights, wounded by a spear, given to Odin, myself to myself, on that tree of which no man knows from what roots it runs.
orig: Veit ek at ek hekk vindga meiði á nætr allar níu, geiri undaðr ok gefinn Óðni, sjálfr sjálfum mér, á þim meiði er manngi veit hvers hann af rótum rennr.
title: Havamal 138-141: Odin's Self-Sacrifice
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en: They gave me no bread, they gave me no drink from a horn. I looked down, I took up the runes, screaming I took them, and I fell back from there.
orig: Við hleiði seldu mér né við hornigi; nýsta ek niðr, nam ek upp rúnar, œpandi nam, fell ek aptr þaðan.
title: Havamal 138-141: Odin's Self-Sacrifice
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en: Nine mighty songs I learned from the famous son of Bölthorn, Bestla’s father, and I got a drink of the precious mead, poured from Óðrerir.
orig: Fimbulljooð niu nam ek af inum frægja syni Bölþorns, Bestlu föður, ok ek drykk of gat ins dýra mjaðar, ausinn Óðreri.
title: Havamal 138-141: Odin's Self-Sacrifice
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en: Then I began to grow wise and to thrive, to grow and to prosper. Word led me to word, from word to word. Deed led me to deed, from deed to deed.
orig: Þá nam ek frævask ok fróðr vera ok vaxa ok vel hafask; orð mér af orði orðs leitaoði, verk mér af verki verks leitaoði.
title: Havamal 138-141: Odin's Self-Sacrifice
author: Anonymous (Norse)

en: King Gylfi was a wise man and skilled in magic. He wondered greatly that the Aesir-folk were so learned that all things went according to their will. He pondered whether this might be of their own nature, or whether the gods they worshipped might be responsible. He set out on a journey to Ásgarð and traveled in secret, having taken on the likeness of an old man as a disguise.
orig: Gylfi konungr var maðr vitr ok fjölkunnigr. Hann undraðisk þat mjök er Ása-fólk var svá kunnigt at allir hlutir gengu at vilja þeira.
title: Gylfaginning 1-3: Creation from Ymir
author: Snorri Sturluson

en: Gylfi came to the hall and saw a man at the door juggling short swords, keeping seven in the air at once. This man asked his name, and Gylfi said he was called Gangleri (“Wanderer”). He said he had come a long way and asked for a night’s lodging. He asked also who owned the hall. The man said it belonged to their king: “I shall take you to see him.” And inside Gylfi saw many rooms and many people, some drinking, some fighting, some playing games.
orig: Gylfi kom til hallarinnar. Hann sá mann í dyrunum er leikaoi með handsaöxum ok hafði sjau senn á lopti.
title: Gylfaginning 1-3: Creation from Ymir
author: Snorri Sturluson

en:
Gangleri said: “What was the beginning? How did it start? What was before?” High One replied: “As it says in the Völuspá: in the beginning there was nothing. Neither sand nor sea, nor cold waves. No earth, nor sky above. Only Ginnungagap, the Yawning Void, and grass was nowhere.”
        Many ages before the earth was made, Niflheim was formed. In its midst lies the well called Hvergelmir, from which flow rivers. The southern region is called Muspelheim—it is bright and hot, blazing and burning, impassable to those who are foreign there.
orig: Gangleri mælti: „Hvat var upphaf? Hvernig hófsk? Hvat var áðr?“ Hár svarar: „Svá sem segir í Völuspá...“
title: Gylfaginning 1-3: Creation from Ymir
author: Snorri Sturluson

s: Chapter 4–5 (Ymir and creation from his body)
en:
Where the warm airs of Muspelheim met the frost of Niflheim in the midst of Ginnungagap, the ice thawed and dripped, and from the drops there formed the likeness of a man. He was named Ymir. He was evil. While he slept, he sweated. Under his left arm grew a man and a woman, and one of his legs begot a son with the other. From these came the race of frost-giants.
        The sons of Bor—Odin, Vili, and Vé—killed Ymir. When he fell, so much blood flowed from his wounds that they drowned the whole race of frost-giants in it. They carried Ymir into the midst of Ginnungagap and made from him the earth. From his blood they made the sea and the lakes. From his flesh the earth was made, and from his bones the mountains. From his teeth and broken bones they made stones and gravel.
orig: Þar er mættusk hitinn ok hrimio ok tók at drjúpa ok renna sem af siðu þeim er ír eldi leyoir... Synir Bors drápu Ymi. En er hann fell, þá hljop svá mikit blóð ór sárum hans at með því druknaðu allir hrímhursar.
title: Gylfaginning 1-3: Creation from Ymir
author: Snorri Sturluson

en: From Ymir’s skull they made the sky and set it up over the earth, with four corners, and under each corner they set a dwarf. Their names are: Austri, Vestri, Norðri, Suðri. From his brains they made the clouds, as it says: “From Ymir’s flesh the earth was fashioned, and from his blood the sea; mountains from his bones, trees from his hair, and from his skull the sky. From his eyelashes the kindly gods made Midgard for the sons of men, and from his brain the hard-tempered clouds were all created.”
orig: Úr hausi hans gerðu þeir himin ok settu hann upp yfir jörðina... Úr holdi Ymis var jörð um sköpuð, en ór sveita sær, björg ór beinum, baðmr ór hári, en ór hausi himinn.
title: Gylfaginning 1-3: Creation from Ymir
author: Snorri Sturluson

en: To God belongs the East and the West. Wherever you turn, there is the face of God. Indeed, God is all-encompassing, all-knowing.
orig: Wa lillahi al-mashriqu wal-maghrib, fa-aynama tuwallu fa-thamma wajhu Allahi, inna Allaha wasiʻun ʻalim.
title: Quran 2:115: Wherever You Turn, There Is the Face of God
author: Quran (divine revelation)

en: And We have already created the human being, and We know what his soul whispers to him, and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.
orig: Wa laqad khalaqna al-insana wa naʻlamu ma tuwaswisu bihi nafsuhu wa nahnu aqrabu ilayhi min habli al-warid.
title: Quran 50:16: Nearer Than the Jugular Vein
author: Quran (divine revelation)

en: I am the Truth.
orig: Ana al-Haqq.
title: Al-Hallaj: Ana al-Haqq (I Am the Truth)
author: Mansur al-Hallaj

en: I saw my Lord with the eye of my heart. I said: Who are You? He said: You. For You, there is no “where,” and no “where” is where You are not. The imagination has no image of You, so that it might know where You are. You are the one who encompasses every “where” until there is no “where”—so where are You?
orig: Raʼaytu Rabbi bi-ʻayni qalbi. Qultu: man Anta? Qala: Anta.
title: Al-Hallaj: Ana al-Haqq (I Am the Truth)
author: Mansur al-Hallaj

en: I have become the One I love, and the One I love has become me. We are two spirits infused in one body. So to see me is to see Him, and to see Him is to see us.
orig: Ana man ahwa wa man ahwa Ana, nahnu ruhani halalna badana. Fa-idha absartani absartahu, wa idha absartahu absartana.
title: Al-Hallaj: Ana al-Haqq (I Am the Truth)
author: Mansur al-Hallaj

s: The Song of the Reed (Book I, Opening)
en: Listen to the reed, how it tells a tale, complaining of separations—saying, “Ever since I was parted from the reed-bed, my lament has caused man and woman to moan. I want a bosom torn by severance, that I may unfold the pain of love-desire. Everyone who is left far from their source wishes back the time when they were united with it. In every company I uttered my wailing note; I consorted with the unhappy and with them that rejoice. Every one became my friend from their own opinion; none sought out my secrets from within me.”
orig: Beshnow az ney chon hekayat mikonad, az jodayiha shekayat mikonad. K-az neyestan ta mara bebrideh-and, dar nafiram mard-o zan nalideh-and.
title: Masnavi: I Died as a Mineral and Became a Plant
author: Jalal ad-Din Rumi

s: The Evolution of the Soul (Book III)
en: I died as a mineral and became a plant, I died as a plant and rose to animal, I died as an animal and I was a human. What should I fear? When have I become less by dying? Yet once more I shall die as a human, to soar with angels blessed above. And even from angelhood I must pass on: all except God perishes. When I have sacrificed my angel-soul, I shall become what no mind has ever conceived. Oh, let me not exist! For non-existence proclaims in organ tones: to Him we shall return.
orig: Az jamadi mordam-o nami shodam, az nami mordam ze hayvani zadam. Mordam az heyvani-o adam shodam, pas che tarsam key ze mordan kam shodam?
title: Masnavi: I Died as a Mineral and Became a Plant
author: Jalal ad-Din Rumi

s: The Bezel of Divine Wisdom in the Word of Adam
en: God wished to see His own essence in a comprehensive object that, having been endowed with existence, epitomizes the whole divine order, so that He might behold His mystery in it. For the seeing of a thing by itself is not like the seeing of itself in another thing, which serves it as a mirror. So the whole affair began and ended with Adam. He is the mirror, and God is the meaning reflected in the mirror. The cosmos was a polished but lifeless mirror before humanity. When God created Adam, the mirror came alive.
orig: Lamma shaʼa al-Haqq subhanahu min haythu asmaʼuhu al-husna allati la yablughuha al-ihsaʼ an yara aʻyanaha—wa in shiʼta qulta an yara ʻaynihi—fi kawnin jamiʻ yahtawi ʻala al-amr kullihi.
title: Fusus al-Hikam: Unity of Being
author: Ibn Arabi

en: The Reality is at once the Seer and the Seen, the Observer and the Observed. The cosmos is His form, and He is the spirit of the cosmos. He is the meaning and the cosmos is His garment. So the cosmos is the outward and God is the inward. God is also the outward, and the cosmos is also the inward. The matter has no beginning and no end—it has always been thus.
orig: Fa-huwa al-marʼah allati yara fiha suratihi, wa huwa al-mirʼah allati yara al-Haqq fiha asmaaʼahu. Wa ma huwa illa ʻaynu dhatihi.
title: Fusus al-Hikam: Unity of Being
author: Ibn Arabi

en: The human being unites in himself the form of the Real and the form of the cosmos. He is the isthmus (barzakh) between the world and the Real, the line where the two seas meet. He is the pupil of the eye of the world, and through him the world gains its sight. Without him, the world would be as a body without a soul.
orig: Fa-l-insan jamaʻa bayna al-surah wa-l-khalifah. Wa huwa al-barzakh bayna al-ʻalami wa-l-Haqq.
title: Fusus al-Hikam: Unity of Being
author: Ibn Arabi

en: Now I shall speak to those who wish to hear of the two primal spirits, who declared themselves as a pair, in thought, word, and deed—the better and the bad. And of these two, the wise rightly choose, not the foolish.
orig: At ta vakhshya ishentu yathanish mazdathanish haurvatatem ashim-cha hyat sravishtā ahurō vahmeng ahubya.
title: Yasna 30: The Two Spirits
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Hear with your ears the best things. Reflect with a clear mind—each one of you—upon the two choices of decision, being aware to declare yourselves to Him before the great retribution.
orig: Sraotā gaeushāish vahishtā avanatā sūchā manangha, āvarēnāu vichithāhyai narēm narem khvakhyai tanuyē, para mazē yaonhē ahmāi nē sazdō pathibyō.
title: Yasna 30: The Two Spirits
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Now the two primal spirits, who revealed themselves in vision as twins, are the Better and the Bad, in thought, word, and deed. And between these two, the wise choose rightly, not the foolish.
orig: At tā mainyū pauruuyē yā yēmā khvafnā asrvatēm, manahiā vachaĭhiā shyaothanā hī vahyah akēm-chā.
title: Yasna 30: The Two Spirits
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: And when these two spirits first came together, they created life and death, and that at the end the worst existence shall be for the followers of the lie, but the best mind for the follower of truth. Of these two spirits, the one who followed the lie chose to do the worst things, but the Most Bounteous Spirit, clothed in the hardest stones of heaven, chose truth—as do those who shall satisfy Ahura Mazda by righteous deeds.
orig: At tā mainyū hamēmīthēm dazdē paouruuyīm gaem-chā ajyāiti-chā, apaēm-chā anghvah anghat apiyēm achīshtahīm drugēvantaeibhyah, at ashavanē vahishtēm manah.
title: Yasna 30: The Two Spirits
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: So may we be those who make this existence brilliant! You Wise Lords, come together with Truth, for there, where understanding converges, is the abode of the Good Mind. Through truth, the glory of Ahura Mazda shall increase, and through good thinking he will give wholeness and immortality. The wise person shall proclaim it.
orig: At chī mahī vah-ishām yōi īm frashyēm kerēnaun ahum. Mazdāonhō anghatū hachā armaitish hachā ashāt.
title: Yasna 30: The Two Spirits
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: May that come which is the better than good—to him who wishes it! Through his sovereign will, may Ahura Mazda grant us enduring strength and truth, and may Good Mind grant us the enduring power through which one may put down hostility.
orig: Ushtā ahmāi yahmāi ushtā kahmāichi. Vasnā fshuyantē dat Ahurō, utayūitī hachaitē ashāt hachā.
title: Yasna 43: Vision of Ahura Mazda
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Thus I realized You as the Bounteous One, O Wise Lord, when I first conceived of You in my mind, when I first beheld You with my eyes—that You are the First and the Last for all eternity, the Father of Good Mind, the true Creator of Truth, and the Lord of existence.
orig: At thvā menghaidī paouryō, Mazdā, yăsmī zī mōi stutō paouruuyīm, hyāt thvā aojyēshē mananghā ashāt-chā.
title: Yasna 43: Vision of Ahura Mazda
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: You spoke to me first, saying: “Go, teach the peoples!” Before that, I was one man; Your word made me many. And this I ask You—tell me truly, Lord—who was the first father of Truth at the birth of creation? Who established the course of the sun and the stars? Who is it through whom the moon waxes and wanes? These things and more I wish to know, O Wise One.
orig: Tat thvā perēsā erēsh mōi vaochā, Ahurā. Kash nā tashāt pāthām ashā ratum? Kash hveng starem-chā dāt advanum?
title: Yasna 43: Vision of Ahura Mazda
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Thus I realized You as the Bounteous One, O Wise Lord, when Good Mind attended me and asked me: “Who are you? To whom do you belong?” And I answered: “Zarathustra am I. A true foe to the follower of the lie, and a powerful support to the follower of truth, shall I be.”
orig: At thvā menghaidī spentam, Mazdā, Ahurā, hyāt mā vohū jaghāt manangha. Peresāt mōi: kahī? Kahē ahī?
title: Yasna 43: Vision of Ahura Mazda
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Thus it is revealed in the Good Religion: Ohrmazd was on high in omniscience and goodness; for infinite time He was ever in the light. That light is the space and place of Ohrmazd. Some call it endless light. That omniscience and goodness are the garment of Ohrmazd. Some call it the Religion. Both are infinite and eternal.
orig: Āghāhīh ī dēn ī weh ēdōn pad-ash rōshaniāgīh. Ohrmazd pad hār-wisp āgāhīh ud wehih ī hamē būd. Rōshnīh gāh ud gyāg ī Ohrmazd. Kē-sh asar rōshnīh xānēnd.
title: Bundahishn 1: Creation Through Thought
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Ahriman was deep in the abyss, in slowness of knowledge and the lust to smite. The lust to smite is his garment. That too is infinite and eternal. Between them was the Void. Some call it the Wind—the place where the two spirits mingle.
orig: Ahriman pad zōfrīh ī āgāhīh ud āz-kāmagih ī hamē būd. Ān āz-kāmagīh jāmag ī ōy. Ān-īz a-kanaragomānd ud hamēwāyag. Miyanīg tōhigīh būd. Kē-sh wāy xānēnd.
title: Bundahishn 1: Creation Through Thought
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Ohrmazd, through omniscience, knew that the Destructive Spirit existed, that he would attack, and that his attack would be mingled with envy. He first created by thought (mēnōg, spiritual form) the creatures that would be needful. For three thousand years they remained in spiritual form—thinking, unmoving, and intangible.
orig: Ohrmazd pad hār-wisp āgāhīh dānisht ku Ganāg Mēnōg hast, ud hameōāwēzēd, ud hamē āwēzēd ī ōy ū arzh bun ahr. Paēstāg dām-ī ān ī abbāyishnīg mēnōgomānd frāz brēhēnīd.
title: Bundahishn 1: Creation Through Thought
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: Then Ohrmazd recited the Ahunwar—the most sacred prayer—and revealed to the Destructive Spirit his own ultimate defeat. Ahriman saw it and was confounded, and fell back into the darkness. He lay stunned for three thousand years. Then Ohrmazd created the world in material form (gētīg): first the sky, then water, then earth, then plants, then the primordial bull, then the first man, Gayomard.
orig: Pad Ohrmazd ahūnwar frāz srūd, ud Ganāg Mēnōg rā ōy frazaft hāmīhā ī xāgīst pad dīd. Kē stūt ud star xward, ud pad tārīgīh ōfrāft.
title: Bundahishn 1: Creation Through Thought
author: Zarathustra (Zoroaster)

en: From the God of knowledge comes all that is and shall be. Before ever they existed he established their whole design, and when they come into being at their appointed time, they fulfil their task according to his glorious design, without changing anything.
orig: מאל הדעות כול הוה ונהיה ולפני היותם הכין כול מחשבותם
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: He created humanity to rule the world, and he placed within him two spirits in which to walk until the time of his visitation: the spirits of truth and of falsehood.
orig: הוא ברא אנוש לממשלת תבל וישם לו שתי רוחות להתהלך בם עד מועד פקודתו
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: In a spring of light is the origin of truth, and from a well of darkness is the origin of falsehood. In the hand of the Prince of Lights is the dominion over all the sons of righteousness; in the ways of light they walk. And in the hand of the Angel of Darkness is all the dominion over the sons of falsehood; and in the ways of darkness they walk.
orig: במעון אור תולדות האמת וממקור חושך תולדות העול
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: These are the counsels of the spirit to the sons of truth in the world: to enlighten the heart of man, to make straight before him all the ways of true righteousness, to make his heart fearful of the judgments of God; a spirit of humility, patience, abundant compassion, eternal goodness, insight, understanding, and mighty wisdom.
orig: אלה עצות הרוח לבני אמת בתבל
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And the counsels of the spirit of falsehood: greed, slackness in the service of righteousness, wickedness and lying, pride and haughtiness, cruelty and great impiety, quickness to anger, abundance of folly, and zeal for arrogance.
orig: ועצות רוח העול
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: Until now the spirits of truth and falsehood struggle in the hearts of men, and they walk in both wisdom and folly. According to his portion of truth so does a man hate falsehood, and according to his inheritance in the lot of falsehood so is he wicked and so abominates truth.
orig: עד הנה נגו רוחות האמת והעול בלב איש
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: Then God will purify by his truth all the deeds of man, refining for himself the body of man, to consume every spirit of falsehood from the midst of his flesh, and to cleanse him through a holy spirit from all wicked practices. He will sprinkle upon him a spirit of truth like purifying waters to cleanse him from all abominations of falsehood.
orig: ואז יברר אל באמתו כול מעשי איש
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: For God has set them in equal parts until the final end, and has put everlasting hatred between their divisions. An abomination to truth are deeds of falsehood, and an abomination to falsehood are all the ways of truth.
orig: כי בשווה שתם אל עד קץ נחרצה
title: Community Rule (1QS) 3.13-4.26: The Two Spirits
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: For the Instructor: the rule of the war. The first attack of the Sons of Light shall be against the lot of the Sons of Darkness, against the army of Belial—against the troop of Edom, Moab, and the sons of Ammon, and against the army of the dwellers of Philistia, and against the troops of the Kittim of Asshur.
orig: למשכיל סרך המלחמה ראש משלחת יד בני אור בגורל בני חושך
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And after the war they shall go up from there and encamp against the king of the Kittim in Egypt. In his time he shall go forth with great fury to wage war against the kings of the north, and his anger shall destroy and cut off the horn of their power. This shall be a time of salvation for the people of God, an appointed time of dominion for all the men of his lot, and of everlasting destruction for all the lot of Belial.
orig: ואחר המלחמה יעלו משם
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: There shall be a great tumult, and the Sons of Light and the lot of Darkness shall fight together for the might of God, amid the roar of a great multitude and the war cry of gods and men, on the day of calamity. It shall be a time of great tribulation for the people whom God shall redeem. Of all their afflictions none shall be like this, from its beginning until its end in eternal redemption.
orig: והיה מהומה רבה ובני אור וגורל חושך ילחמו יחד
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: On the day when the Kittim fall, there shall be battle and terrible carnage before the God of Israel, for that shall be the day appointed from ancient times for the battle of destruction of the Sons of Darkness. On that day the congregation of gods and the assembly of men shall engage, bringing about great carnage.
orig: ביום נפול כתיים תהיה מלחמה
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: The Sons of Light and the lot of Darkness shall fight together in the might of God, with the sound of a great multitude. The sons of righteousness shall shine to all the ends of the earth, continuing to shine until all the seasons of darkness are consumed and, at the time appointed by God, his exalted greatness shall shine for all the times of eternity.
orig: בני אור וגורל חושך ילחמו יחד בגבורת אל
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: For peace and blessing, glory and joy, and long life for all the Sons of Light. On the day when the Kittim fall, the war and destruction shall be great before the God of Israel. For from the God of Israel is the day appointed from of old for the war of annihilation against the Sons of Darkness.
orig: לשלום וברכה כבוד ושמחה ואורך ימים לכול בני אור
title: War Scroll (1QM): Sons of Light vs Sons of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: I thank you, O Lord, for you have redeemed my soul from the pit, and from the Sheol of Abaddon you have raised me up to everlasting height. I walk on limitless level ground, and I know there is hope for the one whom you have shaped from dust for the eternal council.
orig: אודכה אדוני כי פדיתה נפשי משחת
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: You have cleansed a perverse spirit of great sin, that it may stand in the station with the host of the holy ones, and enter into community with the congregation of the sons of heaven. You have cast for man an eternal lot with the spirits of knowledge, to praise your name in the community of jubilation.
orig: ותטהר רוח נעוה מפשע רב להתיצב במעמד עם צבא קדושים
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And I, a creature of clay, what am I? Kneaded with water, what is my worth? What strength have I? For I stood in the realm of wickedness, and with the wretched was my lot. The soul of the poor one sojourned amid great tumult, and calamitous disasters accompanied my steps.
orig: ואני יצר החמר מה אני מגובל במים
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: When all the snares of the pit opened wide, and all the traps of wickedness were spread, and the net of the wretched was upon the surface of the waters; when all the arrows of the pit flew without return and were shot beyond hope; when the measuring line fell upon judgment and the lot of anger upon the forsaken—
orig: בהפתח כול פחי שחת וכול מוקשי רשעה נפרשו
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: Then you opened a fountain for the sake of your glory, and the secret of your truth for the correction of sinful creatures. You purified from sin the one who was cleansed, that he might sanctify himself for you from all abominations of falsehood, and from guilt of unfaithfulness, to be joined with the sons of your truth and in the lot of your holy ones.
orig: ותפתח מקור למען כבודך
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: To raise the worm of the dead from the dust, to the council of your truth, and from a perverse spirit to your understanding; that he might take his stand before you with the everlasting host and the spirits of knowledge, to be renewed with all that is and shall be, and to rejoice together with those who know in a common jubilation.
orig: להרים רמת מתים מעפר לסוד אמתך
title: Thanksgiving Hymns (1QH): Purified from Sin
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: For the heavens and the earth will listen to his Messiah, and none therein will stray from the commandments of the holy ones. Seekers of the Lord, strengthen yourselves in his service!
orig: כי השמים והארץ ישמעו למשיחו
title: 4Q521: The Messianic Apocalypse
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: Will you not find the Lord in this, all you who hope in your hearts? For the Lord will consider the pious, and call the righteous by name. Over the poor his spirit will hover, and he will renew the faithful by his power.
orig: הלוא לא תמצאו את אדוני בזאת
title: 4Q521: The Messianic Apocalypse
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: He will glorify the pious on the throne of the eternal kingdom. He will free the captives, make the blind see, and raise up the bowed down. And forever I will hold fast to the hopeful and in his mercy—and the fruit of a good deed shall not be delayed for anyone. And the Lord will accomplish glorious things which have never been, just as he said. For he will heal the wounded, and resurrect the dead, and proclaim good news to the poor.
orig: ומכבד חסידים על כסא מלכות עד … כי ירפא חללים ומתים יחיה וענוים יבשר
title: 4Q521: The Messianic Apocalypse
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.” Blessedness opened its mouth of wisdom and spoke: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for the kingdom of heaven is theirs.” All the angels and all the saints and everything that was ever born must keep silence here, for they must attend to the speech of the only-begotten Son. I say that blessedness consists in the very thing that the poorest and most destitute person can have.
orig: Beati pauperes spiritu, quia ipsorum est regnum caelorum. Sæligkeit ûf tet den munt der wîsheit und sprach: sælic sint die armen des geistes, wan daz hîmelrîche ist ir.
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

en: If someone asks me what a poor person is who has nothing, I answer thus: as long as a person has his own will, and considers it his will to fulfil the all-lovely will of God, that person does not have the poverty of which I speak. For that person still has a will with which he wills to satisfy the will of God, and that is not true poverty. For true poverty demands this: that a person be so free of all will and of all God’s commands that he would be as free of every divine will as he was when he did not yet exist.
orig: Vrâget mich ieman, waz daz sî ein arm mensche, der niht enhât, sô antwurte ich und spriche alsô:
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

en: In the second place, a poor person is one who knows nothing. We have sometimes said that a person should live as if he did not live, neither for himself, nor for truth, nor even for God. But now I say further: a person must be so free and so emptied that he does not even know or experience that God is at work in him. Only then does he possess poverty.
orig: Ander stünde, der ist ein arm mensche, der niht enweiz.
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

en: The third poverty, and the most extreme, is that a person has nothing. Many people have said that perfection consists in having nothing of earthly goods, and this is true in one sense. But I do not mean it so. I have said before: a poor person is not one who wills to fulfil God’s will, but one who lives in such a way that he is free of his own will and of God’s will, as he was when he was not yet. Of this poverty the highest is that a person so stands empty and free that he does not have even a place in which God might work. For as long as he has that, he still has distinction.
orig: Daz dritte ist daz ergest, daz der mensche niht enhât.
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

s: The Ground of God and Soul
en: When I stood in my first cause, I had no God, and I was my own cause. I willed nothing, I desired nothing, for I was free being and the enjoyment of myself in the likeness of God. Then I willed myself, and I willed all things. And that is what God is. But when I return to God and to the ground where God himself rests in his own nature, that breaking-through is greater than the emanation. For in this breaking-through I receive what God and I have in common. There I am what I was, and there I neither increase nor decrease, for there I am the immovable that moves all things.
orig: Dô ich stuont in mîner êrsten sache, dô enhete ich keinen got, und dô was ich sache mîn selbes.
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

en: The eye through which I see God is the same eye through which God sees me: my eye and God’s eye are one eye, one seeing, one knowing, one love.
orig: Daz ouge, dâ inne ich got sihe, daz ist daz selbe ouge, dâ inne mich got sihet: mîn ouge und gotes ouge daz ist éin ouge und éin gesiht und éin bekennen und éin minne.
title: Sermon 52: The Eye Through Which I See God
author: Meister Eckhart

en: “The just shall live forever, and their reward is with the Lord.” Mark well the word: “the just.” The just person is one who is so formed and poured into the form of justice that he has justice as his very being. Only the person who has abandoned all creaturely things can be truly just.
orig: Iusti vivent in aeternum et apud Dominum est merces eorum.
title: Sermon 6: God Must Become Me
author: Meister Eckhart

en: If anyone were to ask me what a just person is, I would answer: a just person is one who is so conformed to justice that whatever happens—be it joy or sorrow, bitterness or sweetness—none of that matters to him, so long as justice is present in him. Nothing could be more joyful to the just person than justice itself.
orig: Vrâget mich ieman, waz ein gerehter mensche sî:
title: Sermon 6: God Must Become Me
author: Meister Eckhart

s: God and the Soul as One
en: God and I, we are one. By knowledge I take God into myself; by love I enter into God. Some say that blessedness consists in knowledge, some say it consists in love. But I say it consists in neither, but rather in a something in the soul from which knowledge and love flow forth. That something does not know or love. Whoever knows this, knows what blessedness consists in.
orig: Got und ich wir sint ein. Durch bekantnisse nîme ich got in mich; durch minne gân ich in got.
title: Sermon 6: God Must Become Me
author: Meister Eckhart

en: God must become me and I must become God—so entirely one that this “he” and this “I” become one “is” and, in this “is-ness,” eternally perform one work. For this “he” and this “I,” that is, God and the soul, are very fruitful. Not two; one.
orig: Got muoz werden ich und ich muoz werden got, sô gar ein, daz diz “er” und diz “ich” ein “ist” werde.
title: Sermon 6: God Must Become Me
author: Meister Eckhart

en: The Father gives birth to the Son in the soul in the very same way as he gives birth to him in eternity, and no differently. He must do it whether he wills it or not. The Father begets the Son without ceasing, and I say more: he begets me as his Son and the same Son. I say still more: he begets me not merely as his Son; he begets me as himself, and himself as me, and me as his being and nature.
orig: Der vater gebirt sînen sun in der sêle in der selben wîse als er in gebirt in der êwicheit und niht anders.
title: Sermon 6: God Must Become Me
author: Meister Eckhart

en: Since in Christ human nature is united to the divine nature, which is the absolute maximum, it is clear that through Christ human nature has been elevated to the highest. For human nature in Christ is, through union with the maximum, everything that it can be.
orig: Quoniam in Christo humana natura divinae naturae, quae est maximum absolutum, unita est, constat per Christum humanam naturam ad suprema esse elevatam.
title: De Docta Ignorantia III.12: The Coincidence of Opposites
author: Nicholas of Cusa

en: Human nature, elevated in Christ above all the works of God, rests in union with the maximum. Therefore no creature can attain a higher degree of elevation than this: to be united to the maximum in unity of person. And this has been achieved in Jesus.
orig: Humana igitur natura in Christo supra omnia opera Dei elevata in unione maximi quiescit.
title: De Docta Ignorantia III.12: The Coincidence of Opposites
author: Nicholas of Cusa

en: Since the maximum and the minimum coincide, as we have shown, it follows that maximum humanity, which cannot exist without being divinity, is the means by which all human beings can attain the highest union with God. For this is the purpose of the Incarnation: that all might be elevated through him who is the maximum.
orig: Cum maximum et minimum coincidant, ut ostendimus, sequitur quod maxima humanitas, quae sine divinitate esse non potest, medium est.
title: De Docta Ignorantia III.12: The Coincidence of Opposites
author: Nicholas of Cusa

en: Christ is therefore the first-born of all creation, in whom all things were created and in whom all things consist. He is the means through which God descends to humanity and humanity ascends to God. Through him, the maximum descends to the minimum without confusion, and the minimum ascends to the maximum without presumption.
orig: Christus est igitur primogenitus omnis creaturae, in quo omnia creata sunt et in quo omnia constant.
title: De Docta Ignorantia III.12: The Coincidence of Opposites
author: Nicholas of Cusa

en: It follows that every intellectual nature which, through Christ, attains union with the absolute maximum, will reach the perfection of its nature. For the intellect, which desires to know, finds its rest only in the infinite truth. And the will, which desires the good, finds its rest only in the infinite good. In God alone does the intellect know and the will love without any deficiency.
orig: Sequitur quod omnis intellectualis natura quae per Christum unionem cum maximo absoluto attingit, perfectionem suae naturae attinget.
title: De Docta Ignorantia III.12: The Coincidence of Opposites
author: Nicholas of Cusa

en: Since all who are happy are God, it follows that every happy person is God. By nature, indeed, there is one God; but by participation, nothing prevents there being as many gods as you please.
orig: Cum omnes beati dii sint, sed natura quidem unus, participatione vero nihil prohibet esse quam plurimos.
title: Consolation of Philosophy III.10: Every Happy Person Is God
author: Boethius

en: You have shown that the form of the good is such that it contains everything which is sought. But you have proved that happiness is the same as the good. Therefore happiness itself is the same as the divine.
orig: Boni vero formam talem esse monstravisti ut in eo cuncta quae petuntur contineri videantur.
title: Consolation of Philosophy III.10: Every Happy Person Is God
author: Boethius

en: It follows therefore that all who are good are made so by the attainment of goodness. But those who attain goodness become God. Every good person, therefore, is God. But by nature there is only one God; by participation, however, nothing prevents there being as many as possible.
orig: Omnes igitur boni adeptione boni boni fiunt. Qui vero boni sunt dii fiunt.
title: Consolation of Philosophy III.10: Every Happy Person Is God
author: Boethius

en: Therefore this is the condition of human happiness: that when you attain it, you attain divinity. Just as justice makes people just and wisdom makes them wise, so divinity by the same reasoning must make them divine. Every happy person is therefore divine. But while there is nothing to prevent as many as possible from being divine, God by nature is one; by participation, many.
orig: Haec igitur humanae condicio beatitudinis: cum eam adeptus fueris, divinitatem adeptus es.
title: Consolation of Philosophy III.10: Every Happy Person Is God
author: Boethius

en: You have also determined that God and true happiness are one and the same thing. From this we may safely conclude that the substance of God resides in the good itself and nowhere else.
orig: Deum vero bonum ipsum esse ac beatitudinem statuisti. Quare ipsam boni formam dei quoque esse substantiam necesse est.
title: Consolation of Philosophy III.10: Every Happy Person Is God
author: Boethius

en: And the vision was shown to me thus: behold, clouds invited me in the vision and a mist summoned me, and the course of the stars and flashes of lightning drove me and impelled me, and the winds in the vision gave me wings and lifted me upward. They bore me into heaven, and I drew near to a wall which is built of crystals and surrounded by tongues of fire; and it began to make me afraid.
orig: Καὶ ὀράσεις μοι ἐδείχθησαν οὕτως · ἰδοὺ νεφέλαι με ἐκάλουν
title: 1 Enoch 14: The Heavenly Ascent
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And I entered into the tongues of fire and drew near to a large house which was built of crystals. The walls of the house were like a tessellated floor of crystals, and its floor was of crystal. Its ceiling was like the path of the stars and the flashes of lightning, and between them were fiery cherubim, and their heaven was water. A flaming fire surrounded the walls, and its door blazed with fire.
orig: καὶ εἰσηλθον εἰς τὰς γλώσσας τοῦ πυρὸς
title: 1 Enoch 14: The Heavenly Ascent
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And I entered into that house, which was hot as fire and cold as ice. There was no pleasure or life in it. Fear covered me and trembling took hold of me. And as I shook and trembled, I fell upon my face. And I saw in the vision: behold, there was a second house, greater than the first, and the whole door stood open before me, and it was built of flames of fire.
orig: καὶ εἰσηλθον εἰς τὸν οἶκον ἐκεῖνον · θερμὸς ὡς πῦρ καὶ ψυχρὸς ὡς χιών
title: 1 Enoch 14: The Heavenly Ascent
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And in every respect it excelled in splendour and magnificence and size, so that I am unable to describe to you its glory and its greatness. Its floor was of fire, and above it were lightnings and the paths of the stars, and its ceiling also was flaming fire. And I looked and saw therein a lofty throne. Its appearance was as crystal, and the wheels thereof as the shining sun, and the vision of cherubim.
orig: καὶ εἶδον ἐν αὐτῷ θρόνον ὑψηλόν
title: 1 Enoch 14: The Heavenly Ascent
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: And from underneath the throne came streams of flaming fire, so that I could not look upon it. The Great Glory sat upon it, and his raiment shone more brightly than the sun and was whiter than any snow. None of the angels could enter, and none could behold his face by reason of the magnificence and glory, and no flesh could behold him. The flaming fire was round about him, and a great fire stood before him, and none of those around him could draw near to him. And he needed no counsel; the most holy ones who were near to him did not leave by night or depart from him. Until then I had been on my face, prostrate and trembling. And the Lord called me with his own mouth and said to me: “Come near to me, Enoch, and hear my holy word.”
orig: καὶ ὑποκάτωθεν τοῦ θρόνου ἐξεπορεύοντο ποταμοὶ πυρὸς φλεγομένου
title: 1 Enoch 14: The Heavenly Ascent
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: There are two ways, one of life and one of death, and there is a great difference between the two ways. The way of life is this: first, you shall love God who made you; second, your neighbour as yourself; and whatever you do not wish to happen to you, do not do to another.
orig: Ὀδοὶ δύο εἰσίν, μία της ζωῆς καὶ μία τοῦ θανάτου, διαφορὰ δὲ πολλὴ μεταξὺ τῶν δύο ὁδῶν.
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: Now the teaching of these words is this: bless those who curse you, and pray for your enemies, and fast for those who persecute you. For what credit is it if you love those who love you? Do not even the gentiles do the same? But you shall love those who hate you, and you shall not have an enemy.
orig: τούτων δὲ τῶν λόγων ἡ διδαχή ἐστιν αὕτη · εὐλογεῖτε τοὺς καταρωμένους ὑμῖν
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: The second commandment of the teaching: you shall not murder; you shall not commit adultery; you shall not corrupt children; you shall not be sexually immoral; you shall not steal; you shall not practise magic; you shall not practise sorcery; you shall not murder a child by abortion, nor kill one that is born.
orig: Δευτέρα δὲ ἐντολὴ της διδαχης · οὐ φονεύσεις
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: My child, flee from every evil and from everything like it. Do not be prone to anger, for anger leads to murder. Do not be jealous or quarrelsome or hot-tempered, for from all these things murders are born. My child, do not be lustful, for lust leads to sexual immorality. Do not use foul language or be an ogler, for from all these things adulteries are born.
orig: τέκνον μου, φεῦγε ἀπὸ παντὸς πονηροῦ καὶ ἀπὸ παντὸς ὁμοίου αὐτοῦ
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: My child, remember night and day the one who speaks the word of God to you, and honour him as the Lord. For wherever the lordly rule is spoken of, there the Lord is. Seek out daily the faces of the saints, that you may rest in their words. Do not cause division, but rather make peace between those who quarrel.
orig: τέκνον μου, τοῦ λαλοῦντός σοι τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ μνησθήσῃ νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: But the way of death is this: first of all, it is evil and full of cursing—murders, adulteries, lusts, sexual immoralities, thefts, idolatries, magic arts, sorceries, robberies, false testimonies, hypocrisies, duplicity, deceit, arrogance, malice, stubbornness, greed, foul speech, jealousy, audacity, pride, boasting.
orig: Ἡ δὲ τοῦ θανάτου ὁδός ἐστιν αὕτη · πρῶτον πάντων πονηρά ἐστι καὶ κατάρας μεστή
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: See that no one leads you astray from this way of the teaching, since such a person teaches you apart from God. For if you are able to bear the entire yoke of the Lord, you will be perfect; but if you are not able, do what you can.
orig: Ὅρα μή τις σε πλανήσῃ ἀπὸ ταύτης της ὁδοῦ της διδαχης
title: Didache 1-6: The Two Ways
author: Anonymous (Didache)

en: The one who raised me sold me to a certain Rhoda in Rome. After many years, I saw her again and began to love her as a sister. After some time I saw her bathing in the river Tiber, and I gave her my hand and led her out of the river. Seeing her beauty, I reflected in my heart, saying: “I should be happy if I had a wife of such beauty and character.” I reflected on this and nothing more.
orig: Ὁ θρέψας με ἐπώλησέν τινι Ῥόδῃ εἰς Ῥώμην.
title: Shepherd of Hermas: Vision 1
author: Hermas

en: After a certain time, as I was journeying to Cumae and glorifying the creations of God—how great and beautiful and powerful they are—as I walked I fell asleep. And a spirit took me and carried me through a pathless region through which no person could travel, for the place was precipitous and broken up by streams of water. When I had crossed that river, I came to level ground and knelt down and began to pray to the Lord and to confess my sins.
orig: μετὰ χρόνον τινα πορευομένου μου εἰς Κύμας
title: Shepherd of Hermas: Vision 1
author: Hermas

en: While I was praying, the heaven was opened and I saw that woman whom I had desired. She greeted me from heaven, saying: “Hermas, hail!” And looking at her I said: “Lady, what are you doing here?” She answered me: “I was taken up to accuse you of your sins before the Lord.” I said to her: “Are you now accusing me?” “No,” she said, “but hear the words which I am about to say to you. God, who dwells in the heavens and created out of nothing the things which are, and increased and multiplied them for the sake of his holy Church, is angry with you because you sinned against me.”
orig: προσευχομένου μου ἀνοίγη ὁ οὐρανὸς
title: Shepherd of Hermas: Vision 1
author: Hermas

en: I answered her and said: “Lady, if I sinned against you, in what way did I sin? When did I speak an indecent word to you? Did I not always regard you as a goddess? Did I not always respect you as a sister? Why do you charge me falsely, Lady, with these wicked and unclean things?” She laughed and said to me: “The desire for evil entered your heart. Or do you not think that it is an evil deed for a just man when an evil desire enters his heart? It is indeed a sin, and a great one,” she said.
orig: ἀπεκρίθην αὐτῇ καὶ εἶπον
title: Shepherd of Hermas: Vision 1
author: Hermas

en: After she had spoken these words the heavens were shut. I was overwhelmed with sorrow and fear, and said to myself: “If this sin is recorded against me, how can I be saved?” While I was considering these things and debating them in my heart, I saw before me a great white chair made of snow-white wool. And there came an elderly woman in shining garments, holding a book in her hands, and she sat down alone and greeted me: “Hermas, hail!” And I, grieving and weeping, said: “Lady, hail!”
orig: μετὰ τὸ λαλησαι αὐτὴν τοὺς λόγους τούτους ἐκλείσθησαν οἱ οὐρανοί
title: Shepherd of Hermas: Vision 1
author: Hermas

en: Polycarp and the elders with him, to the Church of God sojourning at Philippi: mercy and peace from God Almighty and Jesus Christ our Saviour be multiplied to you. I greatly rejoiced with you in our Lord Jesus Christ that you received the images of true love, and escorted, as was fitting, those bound in chains—the ornaments of the truly chosen of God and our Lord.
orig: Πολύκαρπος καὶ οἱ σὺν αὐτῷ πρεσβύτεροι τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ τῇ παροικούσῃ Φιλίππους
title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna

en: Therefore, girding your loins, serve God in fear and in truth, leaving behind the empty and vain talk and the error of the many, believing in him who raised our Lord Jesus Christ from the dead and gave him glory and a throne at his right hand. To him all things in heaven and on earth were subjected; him every breathing creature serves; he is coming as judge of the living and the dead; and God will require his blood of those who disobey him.
orig: Διὸ ἀναζωσάμενοι τὰς ὀσφύας ὑμῶν δουλεύσατε τῷ θεῷ ἐν φόβῳ καὶ ἀληθείᾳ
title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna

en: Knowing, therefore, that God is not mocked, we ought to walk worthy of his commandment and glory. Likewise, deacons must be blameless before his righteousness, as servants of God and Christ and not of men—not slanderers, not double-tongued, not lovers of money, temperate in all things, compassionate, diligent, walking according to the truth of the Lord, who became servant of all.
orig: εἰδότες οὖν ὅτι θεὸς οὐ μυκτηρίζεται ὀφείλομεν ἀξίως της ἐντολης αὐτοῦ περιπατεῖν
title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna

en: Let us therefore be imitators of his endurance, and if we suffer for his name’s sake, let us glorify him. For he set this example for us through himself, and this is what we have believed. I exhort all of you, therefore, to obey the word of righteousness and to practise all endurance, such as you saw with your own eyes not only in the blessed Ignatius and Zosimus and Rufus, but also in others from among you, and in Paul himself and the rest of the apostles.
orig: μιμηταὶ οὖν γενώμεθα της ὑπομονης αὐτοῦ
title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna

en: I am confident that you are well trained in the sacred Scriptures and that nothing is hidden from you—something not granted to me. Only, as it is said in these Scriptures: “Be angry and do not sin,” and “Do not let the sun go down on your anger.” Blessed is the one who remembers this. And I trust that this is so with you.
orig: πέποιθα γὰρ ὅτι εὖ ἐκτεθραμμένοι ἐστὲ ἐν ταῖς ἱεραῖς γραφαῖς
title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna

en: The Scripture of the Hebrew Exodus has been read, and the words of the mystery have been explained: how the sheep was sacrificed, and how the people were saved, and how Pharaoh was scourged through the mystery. Understand, therefore, beloved: the mystery of the Pascha is new and old, eternal and temporal, corruptible and incorruptible, mortal and immortal.
orig: Ἡ μὲν γραφὴ τῆς Ἑβραϊκῆς ἐξόδου ἀνέγνωσται
title: On Pascha: He Who Hung the Earth
author: Melito of Sardis

en: He is the Pascha of our salvation. It is he who in many people endured many things: it is he who was murdered in Abel, bound in Isaac, exiled in Jacob, sold in Joseph, exposed in Moses, slaughtered in the lamb, persecuted in David, dishonoured in the prophets.
orig: οὗτός ἐστιν τὸ πάσχα τῆς σωτηρίας ἡμῶν
title: On Pascha: He Who Hung the Earth
author: Melito of Sardis

en: He who made the heaven and the earth, and fashioned humanity in the beginning, is proclaimed through the law and the prophets, is made flesh in a virgin, is hung upon the tree, is buried in the earth, is raised from the dead, and is exalted to the heights of heaven. He is the silent lamb, the slaughtered lamb, born of Mary the fair ewe. He was seized from the flock and dragged to slaughter, and sacrificed in the evening, and buried at night.
orig: ὁ τὸν οὐρανὸν ποιήσας καὶ τὴν γῆν καὶ τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἐν ἀρχῇ πλάσας
title: On Pascha: He Who Hung the Earth
author: Melito of Sardis

en: He who hung the earth hangs there. He who fixed the heavens is fixed there. He who fastened all things is fastened on the tree. The Master is insulted. God is murdered. The King of Israel is destroyed by an Israelite hand. O fearful murder! O unheard-of injustice! The Master is disguised, his body naked, and he is not even deemed worthy of a covering that his nakedness might not be seen. For this reason the stars turned and fled, and the day was darkened.
orig: ὁ κρεμάσας τὴν γῆν κρεμαται · ὁ πήξας τοὺς οὐρανοὺς πέπηγεν · ὁ στηρίξας τὰ πάντα ἐπὶ ξύλου ἐστερεώθη · ὁ δεσπότης ὕβρισται · ὁ θεὸς πεφόνευται
title: On Pascha: He Who Hung the Earth
author: Melito of Sardis

en: But he rose from the dead, and mounted up to the heights of heaven. When the Lord had clothed himself with humanity, and had suffered for the sake of the sufferer, and had been bound for the sake of the imprisoned, and had been judged for the sake of the condemned, and had been buried for the sake of the one who was buried, he rose from the dead and cried out: Who is he who contends with me? Let him stand before me. I set the condemned free. I gave the dead life. I raised up the one who had been entombed. Who is my adversary? I am the Christ. I am the one who destroyed death and triumphed over the enemy and trampled Hades underfoot and bound the strong one and carried off humanity to the heights of heaven. I am the Christ.
orig: ἀλλ’ ἀνέστη ἐκ νεκρῶν καὶ ἀνηλθεν εἰς τὰ ὕψη τῶν οὐρανῶν
title: On Pascha: He Who Hung the Earth
author: Melito of Sardis

en: I began to ask myself whether there might be found a single argument which would require no other for its proof than itself alone, and alone would suffice to demonstrate that God truly exists, and that there is a supreme good requiring nothing else, which all other things require for their existence and well-being.
orig: Coepi mecum quaerere, si forte posset inveniri unum argumentum, quod nullo alio ad se probandum quam se solo indigeret, et solum ad astruendum quia Deus vere est.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

en: Therefore, Lord, you who give understanding to faith, grant me that, so far as you know it to be profitable, I may understand that you exist as we believe, and that you are what we believe you to be. And indeed we believe that you are something than which nothing greater can be conceived. Or is there no such nature, since the fool has said in his heart: there is no God? But surely this same fool, when he hears this very thing that I say—something than which nothing greater can be conceived—understands what he hears, and what he understands stands in his understanding, even if he does not understand that it exists.
orig: Ergo, Domine, qui das fidei intellectum, da mihi, ut quantum scis expedire, intelligam quia es sicut credimus, et hoc es quod credimus. Et quidem credimus te esse aliquid quo nihil maius cogitari possit.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

en: For it is one thing for something to stand in the understanding, and another to understand that it exists. When a painter plans what he is about to paint, he has it in his understanding but does not yet understand it to exist, because he has not yet made it. But after he has painted it, he both has it in his understanding and understands it to exist, because he has already made it. Thus even the fool is convinced that something than which nothing greater can be conceived stands in the understanding, since he understands this when he hears it, and whatever is understood stands in the understanding.
orig: Aliud enim est rem esse in intellectu, aliud intelligere rem esse.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

en: And surely that than which a greater cannot be conceived cannot exist in the understanding alone. For if it exists solely in the understanding, it can be conceived to exist in reality also, which is greater. If therefore that than which a greater cannot be conceived exists in the understanding alone, then that than which a greater cannot be conceived is itself that than which a greater can be conceived. But this is obviously impossible. Therefore there is no doubt that something than which a greater cannot be conceived exists both in the understanding and in reality.
orig: Et certe id quo maius cogitari nequit, non potest esse in solo intellectu. Si enim vel in solo intellectu est, potest cogitari esse et in re, quod maius est.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

s: Chapter 3: That God Cannot Be Conceived Not to Exist
en: Indeed, it so truly exists that it cannot be conceived not to exist. For it is possible to conceive of something that cannot be conceived not to exist, and this is greater than that which can be conceived not to exist. Therefore, if that than which a greater cannot be conceived can be conceived not to exist, then that than which a greater cannot be conceived is not that than which a greater cannot be conceived—a contradiction. So truly therefore does something than which a greater cannot be conceived exist, that it cannot even be conceived not to exist.
orig: Quod utique sic vere est, ut nec cogitari possit non esse. Nam potest cogitari esse aliquid, quod non possit cogitari non esse; quod maius est quam quod non esse cogitari potest.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

en: And this is you, O Lord our God. So truly do you exist, O Lord my God, that you cannot be conceived not to exist. And rightly so. For if a mind could conceive of something better than you, the creature would rise above the Creator and judge the Creator—which is utterly absurd. And indeed, whatever else there is, except you alone, can be conceived not to exist. You alone, therefore, most truly of all things have being, and therefore of all things possess being to the highest degree; for whatever else exists does not exist so truly, and therefore has being to a lesser degree.
orig: Et hoc es tu, Domine Deus noster. Sic ergo vere es, Domine Deus meus, ut nec cogitari possis non esse.
title: Proslogion 2-3: That Than Which Nothing Greater
author: Anselm of Canterbury

en: It came to pass, when Jesus had risen from the dead, that he spent eleven years speaking with his disciples and teaching them only as far as the regions of the first commandment and as far as the regions of the first mystery—the mystery within the veil, the mystery of the one and only, the ineffable.
orig: ⲁⲉⲟⲱⲩⲓ ⲇⲉ ⲙⲓⲓ ⲁⲣⲉ ⲁⲥⲓⲩ ⲧⲩⲓⲕ ⲉⲁⲥⲧⲩⲓⲥ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: And Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives with his disciples. And it came to pass that on the fifteenth day of the moon, in the month of Tybi, on the day of the full moon, the sun came forth in its course, and a great power of light descended upon Jesus and enveloped him entirely. He was surrounded by a brilliant, immeasurable light.
orig: ⲁⲥⲩ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲥ ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲙⲉ ⲓⲻⲥ ⲙⲉⲕⲩ ⲣⲉ ⲧⲕⲕⲥ ⲣⲉ ⲕⲥⲕⲥⲓⲓ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: The disciples could not see Jesus because of the great light in which he was. They saw only the light, sending forth light-beams in great abundance, and the light-beams were of different kinds and types, and the light was of every kind. It stretched from below the earth to heaven.
orig: ⲙⲙⲁⲧⲑⲧⲑⲩ ⲇⲉ ⲙⲓⲓ ⲣⲉⲧⲉⲣⲁⲥ ⲉⲓⲁⲥ ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: And they saw Jesus ascending into heaven. And when the disciples saw this, they were greatly afraid and confused. And Jesus, the compassionate and merciful, spoke to them, saying: “Take courage. It is I. Do not be afraid.”
orig: ⲁⲥⲩ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲥⲓⲁⲥ ⲉⲣⲉ ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲉⲥⲑⲓ ⲉⲧⲓⲉ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: And Jesus said to them: “Rejoice and be glad from this hour, for I have been to the places from which I came forth. From this day forward I will speak with you openly, from the beginning of truth to its completion. I will speak with you face to face, without parable.”
orig: ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲇⲉ ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲙⲁⲥ ⲵⲉ ⲣⲁⲱⲉ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲁⲣⲉ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: “I have gone to the height of heights. And I turned to the places of all the archons, and all the rulers, and all the gods, and all the great invisibles. And I went to the places of the great invisible forefather, and to the great triple-powered one, and to the great invisible ones of the thirteenth aeon.”
orig: ⲁⲓⲃⲩⲕ ⲉⲑⲣⲁⲓ ⲣⲉ ⲧⲓⲓⲉ ⲙⲉⲕⲩⲉⲓⲓⲉ
title: Pistis Sophia: Sophia's Fall and Redemption
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: This is the book of the gnoses of the invisible God, by means of the hidden mysteries that show the way to the chosen race, leading in rest to the life of the Father, in the coming of the saviour, the deliverer of souls who receive the word of life.
orig: ⲓⲁⲓ ⲓⲉ ⲓⲵⲩⲩⲙⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲕⲩⲩⲩⲓⲩ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Jesus said to his disciples: “Come to me, all of you, and I will reveal to you the mystery of the emanations and their seals, and their invocations for reaching each treasury. For the Father has given me the authority to reveal these things to you, who are the chosen ones.”
orig: ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲇⲉ ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲙⲁⲥ ⲙⲁⲧⲑⲧⲑⲩ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: “This is the seal of the first treasury: say this name three times. And this is its password. When the guardians of the first treasury see this seal and hear this password, they will withdraw their veils and open the gates, and you will enter in great glory.”
orig: ⲧⲁⲓ ⲧⲉ ⲓⲥⲣⲁⲕⲓⲩ ⲣⲉⲧⲉⲣⲁⲥⲉⲥ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: “And when you have passed through the first treasury, you will come to the second. And the guardians of the second treasury will demand of you the seal and the password of their region. Give them this seal and say this password, and they will open to you.”
orig: ⲉⲧⲉⲧⲉⲣⲓⲉⲣⲩⲣⲉ ⲇⲉ ⲉⲧⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲕⲩⲩⲓⲩ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Jesus said: “Now I will tell you the mystery of the baptisms: the baptism of water, the baptism of fire, and the baptism of the Holy Spirit. These three baptisms are necessary for the one who would enter the treasury of light.”
orig: ⲓ̅ⲩ̅ ⲇⲉ ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲣⲉⲧⲙⲥⲩⲧⲑⲣⲓⲕⲣ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: “The baptism of fire surpasses the baptism of water. And the baptism of the Holy Spirit surpasses the baptism of fire. But the mystery of the spiritual chrism surpasses them all, for it is the seal of the treasury of the great invisible God.”
orig: ⲓⲃⲁⲓⲧⲓⲩⲙⲁ ⲣⲉⲧⲕⲣⲕⲩ ⲣⲉⲧⲙⲩⲕⲩ
title: Books of Jeu: Ascending Through the Heavens
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Mary said: “What is hidden from you I will reveal to you.” And she began to speak these words to them. She said: “I saw the Lord in a vision, and I said to him, ‘Lord, I saw you today in a vision.’ He answered and said to me, ‘Blessed are you, since you did not waver at the sight of me. For where the mind is, there is the treasure.’”
orig: ⲙⲁⲣⲓⲁ ⲇⲉ ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲵⲉ ⲓⲉⲧⲑⲓⲓ ⲉⲧⲃⲉⲓⲓ ⲉⲣⲕⲥ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: “The soul answered the first power, Darkness, and said: ‘What binds me has been slain, and what surrounds me has been destroyed, and my desire has been brought to an end, and ignorance has died. In a world I was set free from a world, and in a type from a heavenly type, and from the fetter of forgetfulness which is not eternal. From this hour on, for the time of the moment, I will receive rest in silence.’”
orig: ⲧⲻⲥⲑ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲕⲥⲕⲩⲃⲉⲓ ⲣⲉⲧⲙⲉⲑⲧⲉ ⲇⲥⲁⲙⲓⲩ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: The soul then came to the second power, which is called Desire. The power said: “I did not see you going down, but now I see you going up. Why do you lie, since you belong to me?” The soul answered and said: “I saw you, but you did not see me, nor did you know me. I was with you as with a garment, and you did not recognize me.”
orig: ⲧⲻⲥⲑ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲉⲓ ⲉⲧⲙⲉⲑⲙⲁⲑ ⲇⲥⲁⲙⲓⲩ ⲉⲧⲉⲧⲑⲧⲑⲙⲓⲁ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: The soul came to the third power, which is called Ignorance. The power questioned the soul, saying: “Where are you going? You are bound by wickedness. You are bound, so do not judge!” The soul said: “Why do you judge me, although I have not judged? I was bound, though I have not bound. I was not recognized, but I have recognized that all things are being dissolved, both the earthly and the heavenly.”
orig: ⲧⲻⲥⲑ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲉⲓ ⲉⲧⲙⲉⲑⲱⲕⲙⲣⲧⲉ ⲇⲥⲁⲙⲓⲩ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: The soul overcame the third power and ascended, and saw the fourth power. It had seven forms: the first form is Darkness; the second, Desire; the third, Ignorance; the fourth, Zeal for Death; the fifth, the Kingdom of the Flesh; the sixth, the Foolish Wisdom of the Flesh; the seventh, the Wrathful Wisdom. These are the seven powers of Wrath.
orig: ⲧⲻⲥⲑ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲃⲓⲉⲣⲕ ⲉⲧⲙⲉⲑⲥⲧⲕⲥ ⲇⲥⲁⲙⲓⲩ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: They questioned the soul: “Where do you come from, slayer of humans? Where are you going, destroyer of spaces?” The soul answered and said: “What binds me has been slain, and what surrounds me has been overcome, and my desire has been ended, and ignorance has died. In a world I was released from a world, and in a type from a heavenly type. The fetter of forgetfulness is temporary. From this hour on, I will attain rest in the silence, having received rest in the time of the moment.”
orig: ⲁⲥⲣⲕⲧⲕⲥ ⲇⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲻⲥⲑ ⲵⲉ ⲓⲉⲧⲉⲣⲩⲥ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲑⲕⲩ ⲣⲉⲧⲣⲉⲥⲁⲓⲁⲥⲩⲓⲩ
title: Gospel of Mary: The Soul Ascending Past the Powers
author: Anonymous (Gospel of Mary)

en: On account of the reality of the authorities, inspired by the spirit of the father of truth, the great apostle—referring to the “authorities of the darkness”—told us that “our contest is not against flesh and blood; rather, the authorities of the universe and the spirits of wickedness.”
orig: ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲑⲥⲕⲩⲧⲁⲩⲓⲩ ⲣⲣⲉⲵⲕⲥⲩⲓⲁ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Their chief is blind. Because of his power and his ignorance and his arrogance he said, with his power, “It is I who am God; there is none apart from me.” When he said this, he sinned against the entirety. And this speech reached up to incorruptibility; and there was a voice that came forth from incorruptibility, saying, “You are mistaken, Samael”—which means, “blind god.”
orig: ⲓⲉⲥⲁⲣⲑⲕⲣ ⲇⲉ ⲕⲥⲃⲁⲕⲣ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲕⲣⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲙⲓⲓ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: The rulers laid plans and said, “Come, let us create a human being out of dust from the earth.” They modelled their creature as one entirely of the earth. The rulers took dust from the earth and modelled their human being, after the body of the rulers and after the image of God that had appeared to them in the waters.
orig: ⲣⲁⲣⲑⲕⲣ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲥⲵⲓⲓⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲕⲥⲕⲉⲓ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲣⲉⲧⲕⲓⲩⲕⲣ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: They said to one another, “Come, let us cause a deep sleep to fall upon Adam.” And he slept. Now the deep sleep that they caused to fall upon him was ignorance. They opened his side, which was like a living woman. And they built up the side with flesh in her place, and Adam came to be entirely of soul only.
orig: ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ ⲙⲓⲓⲉⲥⲉⲥⲣⲉⲥ ⲵⲉ ⲣⲁⲣⲕⲓⲓ ⲉⲧⲕⲥⲕⲉⲓ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲁⲇⲁⲙ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: The spirit-endowed woman came to him and spoke with him, saying, “Arise, Adam.” And when he saw her, he said, “It is you who have given me life; you shall be called ‘mother of the living.’ For it is she who is my mother. It is she who is the physician, and the woman, and she who has given birth.”
orig: ⲧⲓⲓⲓⲙⲁⲧⲓⲕⲕⲣ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲉⲓ ⲉⲧⲕⲥⲧⲩ ⲁⲇⲁⲙ ⲵⲉ ⲓⲉⲵⲁⲥ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Then the authorities came to their Adam. And when they saw his female counterpart speaking with him, they became agitated and lusted after her. They said to one another, “Come, let us sow our seed in her.” But the spiritual woman laughed at them, for they were blind and could not see her. She became a tree, and left before them her shadowy reflection resembling herself.
orig: ⲁⲣⲑⲕⲣ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲥⲉⲓ ⲉⲣⲉ ⲣⲁⲣⲕⲓⲓ ⲉⲧⲃⲉ ⲓⲉⲥⲁⲇⲁⲙ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: Then the female spiritual principle came in the form of the snake, the instructor. And it taught them, saying, “You shall not die a death of dying. For it was out of jealousy that he said this to you. Rather, your eyes shall open, and you shall become like gods, recognizing evil and good.”
orig: ⲧⲓⲓⲓⲙⲁⲧⲓⲕⲕⲣ ⲇⲉ ⲁⲩⲉⲓ ⲑⲣ ⲙⲕⲣⲥⲑ ⲣⲓⲑⲕⲥ
title: Hypostasis of the Archons: The Rulers of This World
author: Unknown (Gospel of Thomas)

en: And the angel of the presence spoke to Moses according to the word of the Lord, saying: “Write the complete history of the creation, how in six days the Lord God finished all his works and all that he created, and kept Sabbath on the seventh day and hallowed it for all ages.”
orig: ወይንገርወ መልአከ መፅናይ መካን ወይቢለው
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “For on the first day he created the heavens which are above, and the earth, and the waters and all the spirits who serve before him: the angels of the presence, and the angels of sanctification, and the angels of the spirit of fire, and the angels of the spirit of the winds.”
orig: እስመ በዓመ ቀዳምት ፈጠረ ሰማያት
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “And the angels of the spirit of the clouds, and of darkness, and of snow, and of hail, and of frost, and the angels of the voices and of thunder and of lightning, and the angels of the spirits of cold and of heat, and of winter and of spring and of autumn and of summer.”
orig: ወመልአከ መንፈስ ዞነት ወአድቢበት
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “And all the spirits of his creatures which are in the heavens and on the earth: the abysses and the darkness, and evening and night, and the light and the dawn and the day, which he prepared in the knowledge of his heart. And then we saw his works, and praised him, and lauded before him on account of all his works; for seven great works he created on the first day.”
orig: ወኩሉ መንፈስ ፈጥረት ዘእመቶሙ
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “On the second day he created the firmament in the midst of the waters, and the waters were divided on that day: half of them went up above the firmament and half of them went down below the firmament, in the midst over the face of the whole earth. And this was the only work he created on the second day.”
orig: ወበዓመ ካልእት ፈጠረ ሰማያት መካከል
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)

en: “On the third day he commanded the waters to pass from off the face of the whole earth into one place, and the dry land to appear. And the waters did so as he commanded them, and they retired from the face of the earth into one place beyond this firmament, and the dry land appeared.”
orig: ወበዓመ ሳልስት አዘዘ ማይት እምፈትናት
title: Jubilees 2: Creation Through Angels
author: Anonymous (Enochic)
